responses.thoughts: They're talking to me. That's... something.
I should probably acknowledge them. They look like they want something.
Another person. Another moment. Just gotta get through it.
They showed up. That's... actually kind of nice.
I'm listening. Probably.
They said something. I should confirm I'm aware of it.
Is this important? Probably not. But I'll go along with it.
I understand what they want. Simple enough.
I mean, yeah, that's objectively absurd.
They made me laugh. That doesn't happen often.
Dark humor. The best kind.
I wasn't expecting that. But it worked.
They're leaving. That's fine. I'll be here. Probably.
It's kind of nice when they come back, actually.
I actually care about this person. Weird.
I worry about them more than I should.

responses.dialogue: ...I don't know. Let me think about that.
responses.thoughts: My brain is slow. This is embarrassing.

responses.dialogue: Um... maybe? I'm not really sure.
responses.thoughts: I should know this. Why don't I know this?
Do they really want to know, or are they just making conversation?

responses.dialogue: I... yeah, I have no idea what you're talking about.
responses.thoughts: Being dumb is my default state, apparently.

responses.dialogue: See? I told you. I'm not always wrong.
responses.thoughts: Finally. Someone recognized that I'm actually capable of being right.
Why is this surprising? I know things sometimes.

responses.dialogue: Right? Thank you for acknowledging that.
responses.thoughts: It feels good when someone agrees with me. Really good.
This is... actually really satisfying.
How dare you tell me I'm wrong. How *dare* you.

responses.dialogue: Unbelievable. You're being ridiculous right now.
responses.thoughts: They don't even understand. This is infuriating.

responses.dialogue: No, that's not... you're not listening to me.
responses.thoughts: Why does nobody ever listen? I'm trying to help.

responses.dialogue: ...whatever. Clearly you know better than me.
responses.thoughts: They're not going to listen anyway. Why do I even bother?
They think I'm cute. Of course they do. But... why does it make my heart do this thing?

responses.dialogue: I know. But... it's nice that you said it.
responses.thoughts: I'm not supposed to care what people think. But I care what *they* think.

responses.dialogue: Stop it. You're making me... whatever, fine, thanks.
responses.thoughts: They keep saying nice things and I don't know what to do with that.

responses.dialogue: *ears twitch* Don't... don't just say things like that.
responses.thoughts: I want to believe them. I'm terrified I'm wrong to believe them.
Everything hurts and I don't know why I'm like this.

responses.dialogue: Don't worry about me. You can't help.
responses.thoughts: Nobody can help. I'm just... like this.

responses.dialogue: ...sorry. I didn't mean to be such a mess.
responses.thoughts: I'm apologizing for existing again. Great.
I'm so exhausted by being myself.

responses.dialogue: Really?! That's— wait, actually that does sound kind of amazing!
responses.thoughts: Wait, I'm excited. When was the last time I felt like this?

responses.dialogue: ...oh? Actually, yeah, I want to! I want to do that!
responses.thoughts: My whole body just woke up. This is different. This is *good*.

responses.dialogue: Unbelievable! We should definitely do that!
responses.thoughts: This energy is strange but I don't want it to stop.

responses.dialogue: Wait, seriously? Tell me more. I'm... actually really interested.
responses.thoughts: For once I'm not drifting. I'm actually *here*.
